codeReview.summary: The feature commit cleanly splits foreign explicit-id persistence from lookup-driven creation. Explicit-id children now route through a dedicated ensure-at-id branch that preserves the declared RecordId, treats MissingTable and NotFound as ensure cases on fresh schemaless graphs, and leaves lookup-driven models on the separate create-based path. The supporting injected-persistence regression was updated to assert the new ensure branch directly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Project instructions require GitNexus impact/detect_changes before editing and committing, but the worker environment did not expose a usable GitNexus command path, so the worker fell back to git diff/code inspection instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The worker handoff records the fallback in discoveredIssues, and the transcript shows git-based inspection around the edit using git diff/git status rather than a successful GitNexus workflow for this feature.

summary: Pass. Commit 48c0f52336b51a982385bc495e23676edb953b94 implements the requested explicit-id ensure split in runtime code and aligns the regression proof with the new branch semantics. No blocking or non-blocking code issues were found in the reviewed feature scope.
